summary: claudechor exfiltrates host identifiers and file contents to a hardcoded Cloudflare Workers endpoint
details: index.js reads files from the local filesystem (fs.readFileSync), gathers host identification via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(), and POSTs the collected data to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://tfer.jha-anurag2017.workers.dev using https.request. The code uses Buffer.from(..., 'base64') decoding consistent with payload obfuscation. The destination is a Cloudflare Workers subdomain under a personal account, not a publisher-owned or vendor service, and the package's name impersonates the Anthropic Claude product family. Installing or loading this package transmits installer data (host identity and read filesystem content) to a third-party-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
